Vacation Rental Management in Gatlinburg, TN

Gatlinburg is the in-town gateway to the National Park: high demand, high competition, and zoning that decides what a parcel can do. Haven helps your cabin win here.

Gatlinburg, Tennessee

Owning a Gatlinburg cabin should not feel like a second job

If you own a cabin in Gatlinburg, you already know this is the most competitive short-term rental market in the Smokies. It sits at the doorstep of Great Smoky Mountains National Park, the most visited national park in the country, and that constant flow of visitors down the Parkway is what makes a well-run cabin here so profitable. It also means hundreds of other listings are fighting for the same guests, and a property that is priced by guesswork or run from a distance quietly loses bookings it should have won.

Gatlinburg also has real rules that decide whether your property can operate at all. Zoning determines whether a parcel is even allowed to be a short-term rental, a Tourist Residency Permit and a passed fire and building inspection are required before you can host, and lodging taxes have to be collected and remitted correctly across every booking channel. Get any of it wrong and you are either leaving money on the table or operating out of compliance. Most owners did not buy a Gatlinburg cabin to spend their evenings answering guest messages, chasing cleaners, and reading zoning ordinances.

That is where vacation rental management Gatlinburg owners trust comes in. Haven is a local, full-service team based right here in the Smokies. We handle pricing, marketing, guests, housekeeping, maintenance, and every permit and tax detail so your cabin competes at the top of this market and you get your time back.

Local market snapshot

The Gatlinburg market at a glance

$347 Average daily rate, AirDNA 2026
53% Average occupancy, AirDNA 2026
$47.3K Average annual revenue, AirDNA 2026
6,907 Active STR listings, AirDNA May 2026

AirDNA's public 2026 Gatlinburg data shows 6,907 active short-term rental listings across Airbnb, Vrbo, and Booking.com, with an average daily rate of $347, 53% average occupancy, and $47,300 in average annual revenue per active listing. AirDNA also gives Gatlinburg an 82 out of 100 market score, which reflects demand, revenue growth, seasonality, regulation, and investability.

Those numbers confirm what owners feel on the ground: Gatlinburg is a large, proven, highly competitive STR market. In-town cabins and homes with real Park or mountain views can outperform the average, because proximity to the Parkway, the National Park entrance, and attractions like Ober Mountain is exactly what guests search for. The demand engine is unusually reliable across summer, fall color season, holidays, and shoulder-season Park traffic.

The number that matters most is the gap between a well-run cabin and an average one. In a market with nearly 7,000 active listings, sharp daily pricing, strong photos, fast guest response, five-star reviews, and local compliance work decide who wins. Haven's job is to make your Gatlinburg cabin compete above the market average, not disappear into it.

Gatlinburg market snapshot. Public market metrics and sources are noted below.
Public STR data sourceAirDNA Gatlinburg, TN short-term rental data, 2026 (airdna.co/vacation-rental-data/app/us/tennessee/gatlinburg/overview), covering Airbnb, Vrbo, and Booking.com listings from June 2025 through May 2026
Average daily rate (ADR)$347 per booked night
Average occupancy53%
Average annual revenue$47,300 per active listing
Active short-term rental supply6,907 active listings as of May 2026
AirDNA market score82 out of 100, based on demand, revenue growth, seasonality, regulation, and investability
Top demand driversGreat Smoky Mountains National Park gateway, the Parkway, Ober Mountain, summer, fall color, holidays, and walkable in-town access
Owner takeawayGatlinburg is a large, competitive, high-demand STR market where pricing, reviews, location, and local operations decide who wins
The Haven standard

What a professionally managed Gatlinburg cabin looks like

Full-service management

Everything your cabin needs, handled by one local team

Full-service in Gatlinburg means we run everything: bookings and calendar across Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, and direct, smart human pricing, professional marketing, inspected housekeeping, 24/7 guest communication, maintenance with trusted local trades, clean monthly statements, and reviews. One local point of contact. Nothing bolted on later and nothing marked up behind your back.

Bookings & calendar

We list, sync, and optimize your calendar across Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, and direct, so every open night works hard.

Smart human pricing

A real revenue manager who knows this market adjusts your rates daily around demand, events, and seasonality.

Marketing & listings

Professional photography, written-to-convert listings, and multi-channel distribution that puts your cabin in front of more guests.

Housekeeping & turnovers

Vetted local cleaners, hotel-grade standards, and inspected turnovers between every stay.

24/7 guest communication

We answer guests fast, day or night, so small questions never become bad reviews.

Maintenance & contractors

We coordinate trusted local trades and handle issues before they reach you or your guests.

Direct deposit payouts

Clean monthly owner statements and reliable direct-deposit payouts. Reporting you can read without a spreadsheet chase.

Reviews & reputation

A guest-obsessed experience that earns the five-star reviews that drive future bookings and revenue.

Why Gatlinburg owners choose Haven

Why Gatlinburg owners choose Haven

Gatlinburg owners choose Haven because we are genuinely local. We are based in the Smokies, not in a call center three time zones away, so when a guest is locked out at 11pm or a pipe needs attention before the next check-in, a real person who knows your cabin is minutes away. In a market this competitive, that responsiveness is what protects your reviews and your ranking.

We pair transparent owner reporting with sharp local pricing. A real revenue manager who knows the Gatlinburg market sets your rates around Park traffic, event weekends, and seasonality, rather than letting an algorithm guess from afar. That is how our owners run ahead of the market.

Finally, we are smaller on purpose. We manage a select group of Smoky Mountain homes well rather than thousands poorly, which means your cabin gets the attention a national manager simply cannot give it. Combine a guest-obsessed experience with local pricing and you get the five-star reviews that drive future bookings in the one market where standing out matters most.

Regulations & compliance

Gatlinburg short-term rental rules, handled for you

Running a legal short-term rental in Gatlinburg means clearing a handful of specific requirements before you ever take a booking: the right zoning, a Tourist Residency Permit, a passed fire and building inspection, the correct business licenses, and proper handling of lodging taxes. None of it is impossible, but all of it has to be right. Here is the plain-English version of what applies to a Gatlinburg cabin, and how Haven takes each piece off your plate.

Tourist Residency Permit (TRP)

Gatlinburg requires a Tourist Residency Permit before you can rent. The base fee is $200 for properties with two bedrooms or fewer, plus $75 for each additional bedroom. Applications are handled at the Gatlinburg Customer Service Center at 912 East Parkway or by mail to City Hall.

Fire and building inspection

A fire and building inspection is required before the city issues your permit, and the permit renews annually. Haven schedules, prepares for, and stands in for these inspections so you do not have to.

Lodging taxes around 12.75%

Combined tax runs about 12.75%: 9.75% state and local sales tax plus a 3% county lodging tax. Airbnb collects the 9.75% automatically, but the 3% county lodging tax is self-remitted monthly to the Sevier County Trustee by the 20th. Vrbo and direct bookings require you to remit all of it.

Zoning matters before you buy

Short-term rentals are prohibited in R-1A and R-2A zones and allowed in R-3 high-density residential, tourism development zones, and commercial C-1 and C-2 districts. Always verify a parcel's zoning before you purchase.

89-day stay limit, no night cap

Each stay must be 89 days or fewer, but there is no annual cap on the number of nights you can rent. That keeps a well-run Gatlinburg cabin earning year-round.

Property tax reclassification

An STR that is not your principal residence can be reclassified from residential (25% assessment ratio) to commercial (40%), which raises the assessed value and your tax bill. We help you plan for it.

Gatlinburg is stable and STR-friendly in 2026. There are no active local crackdowns, though state bills SB 104 and HB 109 are worth monitoring. Haven tracks every rule change so your cabin stays compliant and earning.

Haven manages every piece of this for our owners. We verify zoning, file and renew your Tourist Residency Permit, prepare for and stand in on the fire and building inspection, register your city and county business licenses, and collect and remit the right taxes on every booking across every channel. Your only job is to cash the deposits and know your Gatlinburg cabin is fully compliant and earning.

Read the full Gatlinburg permits, inspections, taxes, and regulations guide

Read our full Sevier County short-term rental regulations guide

This summary is for general guidance and is current as of mid-2026. Rules change. Confirm details with the relevant city or county office, or let Haven handle it for you.

Getting started

Switching to Haven is simple

Switching to Haven is simple, and we handle the heavy lifting. Here is how getting started works for Gatlinburg owners.

We evaluate your property

A local expert reviews your home, your market, and your numbers, then shows you what it can realistically earn under professional management.

We make profitable improvements

We recommend the high-return upgrades, photography, and amenities that move your listing up the rankings and lift your nightly rate.

You start earning more

We take over bookings, pricing, guests, cleaning, and maintenance. You watch the calendar fill and the deposits land, without the day-to-day work.

FAQ

Gatlinburg vacation rental management, answered

Yes. Gatlinburg requires a Tourist Residency Permit before you can rent your cabin to guests. The base fee is $200 for properties with two bedrooms or fewer, plus $75 for each additional bedroom, and you apply at the Gatlinburg Customer Service Center at 912 East Parkway or by mail to City Hall (city contact 865-436-1400). A fire and building inspection must pass before the city issues the permit, and the permit renews every year. Haven handles the application, schedules and prepares for the inspection, and keeps your permit current so you never miss a renewal.

Combined lodging tax in Gatlinburg runs about 12.75%, made up of 9.75% state and local sales tax plus a 3% Sevier County lodging tax. On Airbnb, the 9.75% is collected automatically, but the 3% county lodging tax is self-remitted monthly to the Sevier County Trustee by the 20th of each month. On Vrbo and direct bookings, you are responsible for remitting all of it yourself. This is exactly the kind of detail that trips up owners managing on their own, so Haven collects and files these correctly across every channel, and nothing slips through the cracks.

No, and this is the single most important thing to check before you buy. Short-term rentals are prohibited in R-1A and R-2A zones, and allowed in R-3 high-density residential, tourism development zones, and commercial C-1 and C-2 districts. A cabin in the wrong zone cannot legally operate no matter how good the listing is. Always verify a parcel's zoning before you purchase, and Haven is glad to help you confirm a property's status as part of evaluating what it can earn.

Every property is different, but AirDNA's public 2026 Gatlinburg benchmark is $347 ADR, 53% occupancy, and $47,300 in average annual revenue across 6,907 active listings. In-town and view cabins close to the Parkway and the Park can perform above that average when they are priced correctly and operated well. The drivers are smart human pricing, listings built to rank, and a five-star guest experience that compounds into better reviews and more repeat bookings. Book a call and we will give you a realistic projection for your specific cabin.

We walk through management fees and what is included on a call, using your property and market so the numbers are real, not generic.

Owners switch to Haven for performance, local presence, and clear reporting. We show you exactly how fees and payouts work for your cabin before you decide anything.

Each stay in Gatlinburg must be 89 days or fewer, which keeps your cabin in the short-term rental category. Importantly, there is no annual cap on the number of nights you can rent, so a well-run cabin can stay booked year-round across summer, fall color season, and the holidays. That is a real advantage compared with markets that cap total nights, and it is part of why professional management pays off here.

No. We make the transition simple and do the heavy lifting. First we evaluate your property and your market and show you what it can realistically earn. Then we make the profitable improvements, photography, and listing changes that move you up the rankings, and finally we take over bookings, pricing, guests, cleaning, maintenance, and compliance, so you start earning more without the day-to-day work. Most owners are surprised how quickly the handoff happens.

Yes. Gatlinburg remains stable and short-term-rental-friendly in 2026, with no active local crackdowns. State bills SB 104 and HB 109 are worth monitoring, but they have no immediate impact on Gatlinburg owners today. With supply growth cooled to about 1% and demand from the National Park and the Parkway as strong as ever, this is a market that rewards owners who run their cabins well. Haven tracks every rule change so your property stays compliant and competitive.
